commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds per-tab Settings heights, clamps Settings window sizing against the screen visible frame, sets user resize bounds, and adds SettingsWindowSizing tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main hard-codes every Settings tab to 770 points and resizes without a visible-frame clamp. I did not run the macOS UI in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - The blockers are narrow AppKit sizing math repairs that can be fixed mechanically on the PR branch without a product decision.

target.title: settings: clamp Settings window to visible frame and allow user resize
target.body:
## Summary

The Settings window used a hard-coded 770pt height that ignored the host screen's visible frame, so on 13" displays the bottom of the window (e.g. the **About** tab's "Check for Updates…" button, "Copy Update Diagnostics" button, and copyright row) sat under the Dock. The window was also not user-resizable because `contentMinSize` / `contentMaxSize` were never set on the window — so the user couldn't drag the borders to make it bigger or smaller.

Repro:
1. Open the app on a 13" MacBook (or any display where 770pt + Dock + menu bar > visible height).
2. Open **Settings → About** (or any tab).
3. Observe the bottom of the window is hidden behind the Dock and the window has no resize handles.

### Before vs after (Settings → About, 1440×900 screen, ~807pt visible)

**Before** — window hard-coded to 770pt, content overflows visible frame, bottom rows under the Dock, no resize handles.

![Before](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/0/before.png)

**After** — window auto-shrinks to fit the visible frame, all content visible, resize handles restored.

| Tab | Before window | After window (this PR) |
|---|---|---|
| General | 540×770 (fixed) | 540×540 (clamped to fit) |
| Display | 540×770 | 540×540 |
| Repositories | 980×770 | 980×770 (still big) |
| Accounts | 540×770 | 540×620 |
| Notifications | 540×770 | 540×540 |
| Advanced | 540×770 | 540×600 |
| **About** | **540×770** | **540×560** |

Confirmed live with the patched binary running from `pnpm start` (binary path: `/private/tmp/RepoBar/.build/arm64-apple-macosx/debug/RepoBar.app/Contents/MacOS/RepoBar`):

```
Screen 1440×900, menu bar 24pt → visible frame 1440×807
Settings → About window: 540×648 (incl. chrome), bottom edge at y=837 == visibleFrame.maxY
Dock occupies y=837..900, so the window now stops exactly at the Dock's top edge.
```

## What changed

- `Sources/RepoBar/Settings/SettingsTab.swift`
  - Adds per-tab `preferredHeight` (smaller tabs like **About** no longer get stretched to 770pt).
  - Adds `SettingsTab.minimumContentSize` (420×360) — the absolute floor the user can drag to.
- `Sources/RepoBar/Settings/SettingsView.swift`
  - New pure helper `SettingsWindowSizing` (mirrors the existing `IssueNavigatorWindowController.clampedContentSize` pattern) with `clampedContentSize(desired:visibleFrame:chrome:)` and `minimumContentSize(for:chrome:)`.
  - `resizeSettingsWindow` now:
    - Reads `(window.screen ?? NSScreen.main)?.visibleFrame`.
    - Clamps the desired content size so the resulting frame (content + chrome) fits inside the visible frame.
    - Sets `contentMinSize` / `contentMaxSize` so the user can drag the window edge to a sensible size but never past the screen.
    - Re-anchors the frame so the title bar doesn't jump when switching tabs and the window bottom never crosses the Dock or menu bar.
- `Tests/RepoBarTests/SettingsWindowSizingTests.swift` (new)
  - 8 Swift Testing cases: no visible frame, exact-fit width, Dock-style height clamp, both-axis clamp, degenerate visible frame, negative-chrome safety, `minimumContentSize` chrome subtraction, and lower-bound clamp.

## Commands run

- `pnpm format` ✅
- `pnpm lint` ✅
- `pnpm test` ✅ (632 tests, 105 suites — 8 new)
- `pnpm start` ✅ (app launched from patched build, settings window verified visually + via CGWindowList bounds)
